description: Stilla is an AI agent that quietly keeps track of everything — what was said, what needs doing, and what’s already in motion.

Permissions: Control access at every level. SSO, roles, per-meeting settings, and audit logs.
build_patterns.description: Stilla appears to maintain a permission-aware, shared context graph across tools and meetings, supporting RBAC and entity relationships for collaborative intelligence.

build_patterns.name: Vertical Data Moats
evidence: No training on your data. Your data stays yours. Stilla, and subprocessors don't train on it.
SOC2 (Type 2): Independently audited controls, verified against industry standards.
Trusted by the world's fastest-growing companies
build_patterns.description: While Stilla emphasizes privacy and does not train on user data, the platform's focus on team collaboration and integration with enterprise tools suggests the potential for vertical data moats, especially if proprietary workflows or metadata are used for model improvement.

build_patterns.name: Agentic Architectures
evidence: orchestrating action between humans and AI agents so speed translates to progress
Stilla keeps Linear in sync with what you decide—so you never have to groom issues again
The AI teammate that works, right where you work
Talk to Stilla in Slack, Linear, and GitHub like a teammate
Stilla remembers what you decided across meetings and tools. It updates issues, drafts code, keeps docs in sync, and much more.
build_patterns.description: Stilla is described as an autonomous agent that coordinates actions between humans and other software tools, acting as a teammate that performs multi-step reasoning and orchestration across meetings and integrations.

signals: Integrations with tools like Slack, Linear, GitHub are highlighted
Trust portal and status page are present

unique_findings: Device-level meeting capture: Stilla listens on your device rather than joining calls as a bot. This avoids the common 'ghost participant' problem, improves privacy, and likely requires deep OS-level integration for audio capture and context awareness across all video platforms.

Real-time, collaborative AI notes: Notes are editable live by the team, with Stilla refining them in real time. This suggests a sophisticated backend for multi-user document synchronization and AI-driven summarization, blending collaborative editing (like Google Docs) with AI augmentation.

Persistent, cross-tool context: Stilla claims to 'remember what you decided across meetings and tools' and updates issues, drafts code, and keeps docs in sync. This implies a context engine that unifies data and decisions from disparate sources (Slack, Linear, GitHub, Notion, etc.), which is technically challenging due to data heterogeneity and the need for accurate, persistent memory.

No data training on user content: Explicitly stating that neither Stilla nor subprocessors train on user data is a privacy-forward stance, requiring a technical architecture that isolates user data from model training pipelines—potentially limiting some AI capabilities but increasing trust.

Automated action orchestration: Beyond note-taking, Stilla automates downstream actions (e.g., drafting PRs from conversations, syncing Linear issues, updating Notion, launching Cursor). This is more than summarization—it's workflow automation driven by semantic understanding of meetings, which is a step beyond most AI meeting tools.

story_angles.headline: Inside Stilla’s Agentic Architecture: Building the Multiplayer AI for Teams
story_angles.summary: Stilla is betting on agentic architectures and knowledge graphs to orchestrate seamless collaboration between humans and AI agents. Their platform aims to maintain shared context across tools, a technical challenge most AI SaaS products sidestep. We break down how their approach could redefine team workflows—and where it might fall short.
evidence: Explicit mention of 'agentic architectures' and 'knowledge graphs' as build patterns
Mission to orchestrate action between humans and AI agents, maintaining shared context

story_angles.headline: Vertical Data Moats: How Stilla Plans to Outpace Generic AI with Proprietary Context
story_angles.summary: Stilla’s focus on vertical data moats suggests a strategy to build defensibility through proprietary organizational context. By integrating deeply with enterprise tools and capturing unique team workflows, Stilla could create data advantages that generic LLM-based solutions can’t match.
evidence: Build pattern: 'vertical data moats'
Emphasis on maintaining shared context across an organization's tools

story_angles.headline: Why Stilla Is Doubling Down on Human-AI Alignment—Not Just Automation
story_angles.summary: While most AI SaaS startups chase full automation, Stilla is positioning itself as the 'intelligence layer' that keeps humans and AI aligned. This bet against the pure-autonomy trend could be a differentiator—or a risk if the market moves faster toward agent-only workflows.
evidence: Stilla’s stated mission is to keep humans and AI aligned, not replace humans
Positioning as a collaborative intelligence platform rather than a pure automation tool

story_angles.headline: Can Stilla’s Context Engine 10x Team Productivity—or Is It Just Another Layer?
story_angles.summary: Stilla claims to eliminate the coordination and context-switching that slows teams down, promising a step-change in productivity. But with an engineering quality score of 5/10, can their platform deliver on speed and efficiency, or is it adding complexity?
evidence: Mission to handle coordination and context-switching for teams
Engineering quality score: 5/10 (potential gap between vision and execution)

anti_patterns.description: There is no evidence of proprietary LLMs or unique AI infrastructure; the tech stack is unknown and may rely on third-party APIs, suggesting a potential thin wrapper over existing AI services.
evidence: No mention of proprietary models or technology.
No technical specifics provided about underlying AI.
References to 'agentic architectures' and 'knowledge graphs' are generic and could be implemented via API calls.

anti_patterns.description: The platform's described value—maintaining shared context and orchestrating actions between humans and AI agents—could be implemented as a feature within larger platforms (e.g., Slack, Notion, Microsoft Teams) rather than a standalone product.
evidence: Positioned as 'the multiplayer AI for teams', which is a common feature direction for incumbents.
No evidence of a broad platform or ecosystem; focus appears narrow.

anti_patterns.description: There is no clear data advantage, proprietary technology, or unique technical differentiation visible. The stated 'vertical data moats' are not substantiated with examples or evidence.
evidence: No mention of proprietary data, unique datasets, or exclusive integrations.
Moat described as 'medium' in context, but not justified.

anti_patterns.description: Marketing language is heavy on buzzwords (e.g., 'collaborative intelligence', 'agentic architectures', 'intelligence layer'), but lacks technical depth or concrete product details.
evidence: No technical documentation or product specifics provided.
Claims of 'orchestrating action between humans and AI agents' are not backed by technical explanations.

anti_patterns.description: The market for AI collaboration and orchestration tools is crowded, and Stilla's positioning does not clearly distinguish it from other 'AI for teams' solutions.
evidence: Generic claims about team productivity and AI alignment.
No unique angle or clear differentiation articulated.

competitors.name: Otter.ai
competitors.similarity: Automated meeting transcription, note-taking, and AI-powered summaries.
competitors.how_different: Stilla emphasizes real-time collaborative notes, deep integrations with productivity tools (Linear, GitHub, Notion, Slack), and operates without joining meetings as a bot. Stilla also positions itself as an 'AI teammate' that orchestrates actions post-meeting, not just note-taking.

competitors.name: Fireflies.ai
competitors.similarity: AI meeting assistant for transcription, note-taking, and workflow automation.
competitors.how_different: Stilla does not require a bot to join calls, offers live collaborative editing, and focuses on syncing context and decisions across multiple tools automatically. It also claims stronger privacy (no training on user data) and SOC2 Type 2 compliance.

competitors.name: Supernormal
competitors.similarity: AI-powered meeting notes, summaries, and integrations with productivity tools.
competitors.how_different: Stilla's differentiation is in its 'multiplayer' approach—shared live docs, team collaboration during meetings, and persistent context across tools and threads. It also highlights more advanced post-meeting automation (e.g., updating Linear, drafting PRs, updating Notion, etc.).

competitors.name: Fathom
competitors.similarity: Automated meeting summaries, transcripts, and CRM integrations.
competitors.how_different: Stilla positions itself as an orchestration layer for both humans and AI agents, not just a meeting summary tool. It integrates more deeply with engineering and product tools, and supports real-time collaboration.

competitors.name: Notion AI
competitors.similarity: AI-powered documentation, note-taking, and knowledge management.
competitors.how_different: Stilla is focused on meetings and action orchestration, not just documentation. It claims to keep context in sync across multiple tools and workflows, acting as an active teammate rather than a passive knowledge base.

differentiation.primary: Stilla acts as a 'multiplayer AI teammate' that maintains shared context, orchestrates actions across tools, and enables real-time team collaboration before, during, and after meetings.
differentiation.technical: Runs locally (no bot required in meetings), offers live collaborative notes, integrates deeply with a wide range of productivity/developer tools, maintains persistent context across meetings and threads, SOC2 Type 2 compliance, and does not train on user data.
differentiation.business: Focuses on high-velocity, product-centric teams (startups to public companies), offers a free trial, and positions itself as a central intelligence layer for teams. Emphasizes privacy and security as a selling point.
differentiation.positioning: Stilla is positioned as the 'AI teammate' for teams, not just a meeting assistant. It claims to go beyond transcription/notes to automate follow-up actions, keep context in sync, and reduce coordination overhead.

secret_sauce.core_advantage: Stilla's unique advantage is its ability to maintain and orchestrate shared context across an organization's tools, enabling seamless collaboration and automated action from meetings to execution—without bots joining calls.
secret_sauce.defensibility: This is hard to replicate due to the breadth and depth of integrations, real-time collaborative architecture, privacy-first approach (no data used for training), and the persistent context engine that links conversations to actions across tools.
evidence: Works on all video tools without bot—Stilla listens on your device, so no bot ever joins the call.
Collaborative AI notes—One shared doc for notes and decisions. Your team edits live. Stilla refines as you go.
Stilla keeps Linear in sync with what you decide—so you never have to groom issues again.
Talk to Stilla in Slack, Linear, and GitHub like a teammate. Your whole team can join the same thread. Stilla remembers what you decided across meetings and tools.
No training on your data—Your data stays yours. Stilla, and subprocessors don't train on it.
SOC2 (Type 2)—Independently audited controls, verified against industry standards.

competitive_analysis.moat_explanation: Stilla's competitive position is defensible due to its privacy-centric architecture (no bots, no training on user data), deep and broad integrations, and real-time collaborative features. However, the meeting AI space is crowded, and larger incumbents or well-funded startups could replicate integrations and workflow automation. The persistent context and orchestration layer provide some defensibility, but ongoing innovation and ecosystem lock-in will be necessary to maintain a strong moat.

evidence_quotes: Stilla takes meeting notes. And does the work.
